target.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
target.assumption: Big Bank refers to JPMorgan Chase (JPM)

results.headline_summary: JPMorgan delivered a strong 1Q26 with broad-based strength across CCB, CIB, and AWM, while maintaining solid capital and liquidity metrics.
risks: NII sensitivity to rates, deposit costs, and funding mix
actionable_takeaways: Quality remains the core thesis: JPM is still the scale leader with strong profitability and a well-capitalized balance sheet.
The 1Q26 print supports the bull case with double-digit revenue, net income, and EPS growth plus healthy NII growth.
CCB, CIB, and AWM all contributed to the upside, indicating broad franchise strength rather than one-off performance.
CET1 at 14.3% and LCR at 112% indicate the balance sheet remains resilient.
Monitor NII, credit losses, deposits, and any change in valuation discipline after the strong quarter.
data_quality_notes: Fields without values in the readout were set to null.
Day change / intraday move was not provided in the readout.
Enterprise value and dividend payout ratio were not provided in the readout.
results.full_report:
# Big Bank Noon Sweep — 2026-05-26 12:00 PM PDT

**Big Bank assumption:** JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM)

## Market / valuation
- Price: $305.81
- Market cap: ~$825.4B
- P/E: 14.9x
- P/B: 2.28x
- ROE: 19%

## 1Q26 financial highlights
- Revenue: $49.84B (+10% YoY)
- Net income: $16.49B (+13% YoY)
- EPS: $5.94 (+17% YoY)
- Net interest income: $25.37B (+9% YoY)

## Balance sheet / capital
- Assets: $4.90T
- Deposits: $2.68T
- Loans: $1.50T
- CET1: 14.3%
- LCR: 112%

## Segment highlights
- CCB net revenue: $19.57B (+7% YoY)
- CIB net revenue: $23.38B (+19% YoY)
  - Investment banking: +28% YoY
  - Markets: +20% YoY
- AWM net revenue: $6.37B (+11% YoY)
  - AUM: $4.8T (+16% YoY)

## Risks
- NII sensitivity to rates and deposit costs
- Credit-cycle / loan-loss risk
- Valuation compression if expectations reset
- Capital / regulatory constraints
- Markets and investment-banking cyclicality

## Actionable takeaways
- JPM remains the quality leader in the Big Bank set.
- 1Q26 showed strong across-the-board growth.
- Capital and liquidity remain solid.
- CCB, CIB, and AWM all contributed positively.
- Watch NII, credit, deposits, and valuation discipline going forward.
